t_ch: 今日我心神不宁、集中不了注意力…这实在是不妙。
t_en: I feel restless and distracted today... This is bad.

t_ch: 卜者应该相信客观事实，过于情绪化只会影响占算结果…您倘若不能理解，我问您一个问题您便清楚了，人为什么想要占卜第二次呢？
t_en: Diviners should believe in objective facts, so being too emotional will affect the result. Let me ask you a question, and you'll be able to understand it. Why would people want to do divination for the second time?

t_ch: 我怀疑第一次的结果。
t_en: Because they have doubts about the first result.

t_ch: 不错，这正是占卜第二次的直接原因。
t_en: Exactly. That's the direct reason.

t_ch: 我想要验证第一次的结果。
t_en: Because they want to verify the first result.

t_ch: 说得好听，不就是对第一次占卜结果产生了怀疑了吗？
t_en: It's just a nice way to put it. Doesn't it show that they have doubts about the first result?

t_ch: 我想改变先前的结果。
t_en: Because they want to change the previous result.

t_ch: 占卜只是在描述「未来的事实」，再次卜算也改变不了，您只是在怀疑罢了。
t_en: Divination only describes upcoming facts, which can't be changed by another divination. They just refuse to believe the result.

t_ch: 曾经一位好友曾如此告诉我「对占算结果充满怀疑，企图二次占卜，这是卜者的大忌」。
t_en: A friend of mine once told me that it is taboo for diviners to doubt the results and try to divine twice.

t_ch: 但可笑的是，她却因为准确的卜算离开了太卜司，实在是令人唏嘘。
t_en: Ironically, she left the Divination Commission because of her accurate divination. This is really saddening.

t_ch: 不好意思，我有些离题万里了…我认得您，您是符玄大人委以重任的英雄，一点见面礼，不成敬意。请问还有什么事情找我吗？
t_en: Forgive me for going off-topic here... I've seen you before. You are the hero whom Master Fu Xuan regards highly. Here is a small gift for you. Is there anything else I can do for you?

t_ch: 想跟您打听一些事儿…
t_en: I'd like to ask you a few things...

t_ch: 您尽管问，不过我知道也有有限，太复杂的事情，就麻烦您去问问几位卜官大人了。
t_en: Ask away, though I don't know much. If it's about something too complicated, I'm afraid you'll have to ask the Managing Diviners.

t_ch: 想问问您那位朋友的事…
t_en: I'd like to ask about your friend...

t_ch: 那位卜者叫作若月，您可能在长乐天广场上见过她的占卜店「鉴爻轩」，从太卜司里离职之后，就回去继承家业了。
t_en: Her name is Ruoyue, and you've probably passed by her divination shop in the Exalting Sanctum Plaza called the Looking Bronze. After leaving the Divination Commission, she went back home to take over her family business.

t_ch: 建木之灾前她就算出「卦象涨落在艮、坎之间，其势大凶」，然而突如其来的卜卦结果既没能指出时间因由，也无法重现，没有卜官能够采信她。
t_en: Before the disaster in Ambrosial Arbor happened, she already saw the trigram vacillate between the Mountain and Water trigrams to a terrible degree. Yet, the sign that came suddenly didn't point to a specific time and reason and couldn't be shown again. Therefore, no diviners took her divination seriously.

t_ch: 若月后来也因为各种原因离开了这里，她说自己满足于现在的生活，哎，我不知道这话里有几分真心。
t_en: Later, Ruoyue left the Commission due to various reasons. She said she was satisfied with her current life, but I'm not sure she meant it.

t_ch: 预测不是立即做出改变的保证。
t_en: Forecasting doesn't guarantee immediate changes.

t_ch: 「而是提供人们做出更明智决策的基础」，我明白，我只是…有些遗憾。
t_en: "It's to provide a basis for people to make a wiser decision." I understand, but... I just feel somewhat sorry for it.

t_ch: 说不定离开这里也是一桩好事。
t_en: Perhaps, it's a good thing to leave here.

t_ch: 究竟是不是好事，我不知道。
t_en: I'm not sure about that.

t_ch: 但我看她离职之后眼睛里有光，是比不少眼神涣散的卜者要健康不少的。
t_en: But I can see her eyes sparkling after quitting her job. She seems healthier than those diviners who always look lethargic.

t_ch: 如果当时司部收到了报告，又会是怎样的结果呢？哎，卜者不应谈论「如果」。
t_en: What would happen if the commission received her report back then? Alas, I shouldn't be talking about the what-ifs as a diviner.

t_ch: 太卜司的职场环境好像有点…
t_en: The Divination Commission as a workplace is so...

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，您是想说「严峻」对吗？那是您多虑了。
t_en: Hahahaha, do you want to say "harsh"? That's not really the case.

t_ch: 三位卜官大人的想法人尽皆知，太卜大人也没有挑明，只有他们仨还以为别人不知道…看他们在职场上明争暗斗也属实是一件有趣的事。
t_en: Everyone knows what's on the minds of the three Managing Diviners. As the master diviner doesn't address the issue directly, the three still assume that the competition between them is a secret... It's quite amusing to see them scheming against each other at work.

t_ch: 至于若月，听说她被符玄大人算出「她会离开太卜司」，于是便让她离开了。
t_en: About Ruoyue, I heard that Lady Fu Xuan had predicted her departure from the Divination Commission before, so she allowed her to leave.

t_ch: 若月有关建木之灾的卜测报告让三位卜官脸上挂不住，说不定「离职」是太卜大人的一种保护方法…当然，这只有太卜大人自己才知道了。
t_en: The Managing Diviners were left ashamed because of Ruoyue's divination about the Ambrosial Arbor disaster. Perhaps, letting her go is the master diviner's way to protect her... Of course, no one can be sure about this except the master diviner herself.

t_ch: 听说你们和工造司的人不对付？
t_en: I heard that you guys are not on good terms with those from the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 不利于团结的事情还是不要说了。
t_en: Let's not talk about things that will undermine the spirit of unity.

t_ch: 他们建木受灾还得靠太卜司协助做清算，这说明我们之间关系还是挺好的。
t_en: We helped them to calculate the damage after the Ambrosial Arbor disaster. This shows that our relationship is fine.

t_ch: 不过我还得适当地批评一下他们，他们那些匠人老在外面传「太卜司工作清闲、喝喝茶水一天就过了」……
t_en: Still, I must criticize them for telling everyone that working in the Divination Commission is a breeze, and we spend most of the time enjoying snacks...

t_ch: 世界上哪有这种好事！这种话，我们自己调侃一下自己就算了，有一天我收到朋友发来信息问我「卜者真的有么清闲吗」，我真是无言以对。
t_en: There is no such thing in this world! When we say that about ourselves, we mean it as a joke. One day, I received a text message from a friend asking me whether diviners were really so free. I was speechless.

t_ch: 还得是他们工造司项目多、收入高，随便搞个发明就能躺在专利费上舒舒服服地混上几十年，这不比太卜司好多了？
t_en: Those at the Artisanship Commission handle lots of projects and are paid well. With just one invention, they can live off the royalties for decades. Isn't it much better than the job at the Divination Commission?

t_ch: 没有其他想问的事儿了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 好的，您慢走。
t_en: Okay. See you.

t_ch: 没有其他想问的事儿了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 好的，您慢走。
t_en: Okay. See you.

t_ch: 要是若月在这里就好了，她倒是比我能掐会算……
t_en: If only Ruoyue was here... She's a better diviner than me...

t_ch: 您来了，请问还有什么事情要找我吗？
t_en: There you are. Is there anything else I can do for you?

t_ch: 想跟您打听一些事儿…
t_en: I'd like to ask you a few things...

t_ch: 您尽管问，不过我知道也有有限，太复杂的事情，就麻烦您去问问几位卜官大人了。
t_en: Ask away, though I don't know much. If it's about something too complicated, I'm afraid you'll have to ask the Managing Diviners.

t_ch: 想问问您那位朋友的事…
t_en: I'd like to ask about your friend...

t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 好的，您慢走。
t_en: Okay. See you.

t_ch: 没有其他想问的事儿了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 好的，您慢走。
t_en: Okay. See you.

t_ch: 嘁，我不知道这些破店有什么好，都是些不值钱的破烂嘛…噢，我可不是在说您。
t_en: Tsk, I just don't get what's so great about these shabby stores. Worn-out trash for low-end customers... Oh, I'm not talking about you.

t_ch: 你的评价真苛刻啊。
t_en: That was pretty harsh.

t_ch: 哼，抱歉。但这不过是有话直说罢了。
t_en: Well, then I'm sorry for being real.

t_ch: 要知道长生种的社会里，平凡无奇的琐碎物件会成为外界的历史文物。不过嘛，在公司专家的眼中，这些不过是过时的破烂而已。
t_en: In the society of long-life species, some mundane and trivial objects may hold great historical value for Outworlders. However, in the eyes of IPC elites, these objects are nothing more than outdated trash.

t_ch: 我觉得这些东西挺值钱的。
t_en: But I think this stuff is worth quite a lot of money.

t_ch: 值钱？值钱就怪了！您恐怕并不了解银河商业的潮流，就别凑这热闹了。
t_en: Quite a lot of money? Come on, are you even serious? If you don't know the latest trend of cosmic business, you might as well not say anything at all.

t_ch: 这金人巷要是打算继续卖破烂，我劝这里的商户赶紧破产算了。
t_en: If Aurum Alley keeps selling junk like this, those merchants won't be in business for long, at least not if I have anything to say about it.

t_ch: 忘了自我介绍了，我叫法尔杜丝，第一次见面，如果您有玉兆，我可以发送名帖给您，顺带送上一些商务伴手礼。
t_en: Oh, by the way, my name is Fardus. Nice to meet you. If you happen to have a jade abacus, I'd be happy to send you an invitation letter and some gifts.

t_ch: 我是星际和平公司的商业规划专家，这次随团参加公司在金人巷相关的事务。如果有任何业务咨询和合作意向，请随时和我联系。
t_en: I'm an IPC business planning specialist, and I'm here on business affairs related to Aurum Alley. Feel free to contact me if you have any business inquiries or are interested in cooperating with us.

t_ch: 一看您就比仙舟的老古董好说话，让我们进行一场「现代的交流」吧。
t_en: You seem way more reasonable than those old chumps. So, how about we have a real chat? You know, a "modern" one.

t_ch: 你说这些店铺是卖破烂的？
t_en: Did you just say these stores are selling worn-out trash?

t_ch: 没错，挺好理解的，对吧？这些落后时代的老家伙，开着落后时代的破店，卖着落后时代的破烂，有任何必要吗？
t_en: Yeah, that's exactly what I said. Those old geezers are stuck in the past, running outdated stores and selling useless junk. I mean, what's even the point?

t_ch: 前段时间，地区主管让我们去给现有商铺做估值，然后与商户们商量一下预期转让金额，没想到这些老家伙狮子大张口……
t_en: Some time ago, the regional administrator wanted us to assess the value of the stores in the alley and negotiate with the owners on how much they'd want if we decided to buy them out. But those old geezers just threw out a ridiculously high number...

t_ch: 你们打算把这里买下来？
t_en: So, IPC is planning to buy this block?

t_ch: 当然了，地区主管想把这里打造成商业港口，我觉得这里可以建立起大型购物中心，无论往哪个方向建设，总得赶走这些老店铺。
t_en: Exactly, we're planning to turn this area into a commercial hub with a large shopping center. But first, we've got rid of those run-down stores.

t_ch: 星际和平公司缺钱吗？
t_en: Is IPC short on cash or something?

t_ch: 公司自然是不会缺钱的，但我们花出去的每一笔账都只为公司的发展负责，不会为「历史价值」埋单。
t_en: No, of course not, but we'll only pay for things that actually help IPC move forward and get ahead, and definitely not just because of "historical values."

t_ch: 公司不会花重金买破烂的，所以我的工作还得继续做。
t_en: We're not wasting money on worthless junk. That's why I'm here to make sure everything is legit.

t_ch: 公司打算买下金人巷？
t_en: So, is IPC planning to buy Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 准确来说，是金人巷的经营权。
t_en: To be more precise, we are planning to obtain the right to operate this alley.

t_ch: 公司要对金人巷不利？
t_en: Is IPC planning to undermine Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 不利？您有被迫害妄想吗？
t_en: Come on, what makes you think that? Do you have a persecution complex or something?

t_ch: 公司是来建设金人巷的，不是来破坏金人巷的。金人巷只会比以前更有价值，我看{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}你有些见识，别跟老古董一般见识呀。
t_en: We're here to develop this alley, not destroy it. The new Aurum Alley is only going to get better. Don't be like those stubborn folks, {F#young lady}{M#young man}.

t_ch: 公司所做的一切都是为了利…啊，不对，是为了伟大的克里珀！
t_en: Everything we've been doing is for profi... I mean, for the profound Qlipoth!

t_ch: 商户们会接受吗？
t_en: Will those merchants accept your offer?

t_ch: 接受？他们有选择么？
t_en: Will they accept? Well, do they really have a choice?

t_ch: 公司与仙舟再次恢复稳定的贸易联系之后，天舶司提供了商贸合作的范围，金人巷地区的经营管理也在招标文件上。
t_en: After the IPC re-established stable trade routes with the Xianzhou, the Sky-Faring Commission had outlined the scope of the business cooperation with the management for Aurum Alley also being up for negotiation in the tender documents.

t_ch: 您明白了吧？这，可是正儿八经的商业竞争。
t_en: Do you understand now? We're talking about a bona fide business competition.

t_ch: 我听说公司有些业务员急功近利，耍弄小手段，真丢人。
t_en: I heard that some of our sales representatives are resorting to shady tactics to get what they want. What a disgrace.

t_ch: 拿下金人巷，根本不需要奇技淫巧，实力差距决定了结果。正如克里珀的锤子终将下落！
t_en: We don't need any underhanded tricks to claim Aurum Alley. The sheer difference in strength we bring will guarantee our victory, just like Qlipoth's hammer is guaranteed to fall!

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 慢走啊，希望日后有机会与您合作。
t_en: Take your time. I hope we'll get to work together in the future.

t_ch: 又是被老古董们气倒的一天，长生种怎么会这么固执？
t_en: Ah, another day of being annoyed by those old geezers... Why are long-life species so stubborn?

t_ch: …呃？你是慕名而来的游客吗？这种地方到底有什么好逛的…还是说你是来咨询的？
t_en: ...Huh? Another tourist lured in by those ads? What's so great about this rundown alley anyways... Or are you here to ask something?

t_ch: 呃？您是慕名而来的游客吗？这种地方到底有什么好逛的……
t_en: ...Huh? Another tourist lured in by those ads? What's so great about this rundown alley anyways...

t_ch: 不记得我啦？
t_en: You don't remember me?

t_ch: 这地方多好呀。
t_en: What do you mean? This place is great.

t_ch: 我有些印象，金人巷商会在你的帮助下拿下了地区经营权。
t_en: I think I remember you. The Aurum Alley Merchant Guild got the rights to operate this area with your help.

t_ch: 所以，您来找我做什么呢？
t_en: So, what do you want from me?

t_ch: 现在就想跟您咨询一下。
t_en: I'd like to ask something.

t_ch: 别这么客气，想问什么就问吧。
t_en: Please, don't be a stranger. Ask whatever you want.

t_ch: 公司怎么还不离开金人巷？
t_en: Why is the IPC still staying in Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 可以理解，您和这里的商户一样，对公司充满了敌意。
t_en: I understand why you'd be asking that. You're just like the other merchants here, all hostile towards the IPC.

t_ch: 不过咱们在这里经营的业务很广，可不只有金人巷一处。
t_en: But, as you know, the IPC operates many businesses throughout the Luofu, far exceeding the borders of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 虽然这一次的结果没能达成「完全自主经营」的目的，但这个结果也算是被上层意料到了。得失不在一城一池，真正的战斗还在未来。
t_en: While we failed to achieve absolute commercial autonomy here this time, the result was not necessarily a surprise for the higher-ups. Having a lost a battle doesn't mean we've lost the war. The real contest lies in the future.

t_ch: 至于是多远的未来，我未必看得到。
t_en: As for how far into the future... Maybe I won't even be around to see it.

t_ch: 我看你还是不喜欢金人巷。
t_en: I can tell you're still not a fan of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 小吃不错，仅止于此。
t_en: The snacks here are great, and that's all there is.

t_ch: 要是这里建立起大型购物中心，「尚滋味」和「美馔阁」或许会有位置的。
t_en: If we were to build a large shopping mall here, perhaps we would reserve space for Spices Supreme and Delicacy Pavilion.

t_ch: 而剩下的商户在我看来，都是些卖破烂的…关于这点，我的想法没有变。
t_en: But, to be honest, the other merchants here are just selling junk. My opinion on that hasn't changed a bit.

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 慢走啊，希望日后有机会与您合作。
t_en: Take your time. I hope we'll get to work together in the future.

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 慢走啊，希望日后有机会与您合作。
t_en: Take your time. I hope we'll get to work together in the future.

t_ch: 现在就想跟您咨询一下。
t_en: I'd like to ask something.

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 不会吧，你不会是在盯着我吧？哟，我们可是正——经——商会。你先别走，要是给不出个像样的说法，你就摊上事了。
t_en: Hold up. You're not staring at me, are you? Hey, we're a respectable merchant guild over here. Don't ya think of running away. Gimme one good reason for what you've done, or you'll be in big trouble.

t_ch: 我是觉得你好看。
t_en: Cos you look pretty.

t_ch: {F#小老~妹}{M#小老~弟}挺会说话，跟我第一次见面就说我好看，我只能说你太过虚——伪了呀，无意冒犯，我也是一个虚伪的人。
t_en: A honey-tongued {F#lass}{M#lad}, aren't you? This is the first time we've met and you're already saying I look good. What a HYPOCRITE. No offense though. I'm a hypocrite, too.

t_ch: 瞅你怎么了？就瞅你。
t_en: So? Imma look at whatever I want.

t_ch: {F#小老~妹}{M#小老~弟}说话还挺横的。这要在我的地界上，非得让你明白真志摩的规矩，不过嘛——这是仙舟罗浮，凡事讲究个「以和为贵」。
t_en: Tough {F#lass}{M#lad}, I see. We aren't on my turf, otherwise you'll be getting a good taste of my fists. But we're on the Xianzhou Luofu, so let's just follow their principle of "harmony" or whatever.

t_ch: 具体说说我摊上了什么事？
t_en: Care to tell me what trouble I'll in?

t_ch: 看来{F#小老~妹}{M#小老~弟}是个听不懂人话，也不珍惜生命的家伙，哈哈——跟我一样，有点意思，我俩是同一个类型的人。
t_en: Oh! A bodacious {F#lass}{M#lad} who has a death wish and never listens. Haha, how interesting! We're more alike than you think.

t_ch: 我只是路过，没有在看你。
t_en: I wasn't looking at you. I was just passing by.

t_ch: 可别想糊弄我呀，{F#小老~妹}{M#小老~弟}。
t_en: Oh, don't try to muddle through, {F#lass~}{M#lad~}.

t_ch: 人在说谎的时候，流汗的味道也不一样，*嗅嗅*我嗅觉很灵敏的，你这味道……
t_en: People's sweat smells different when they're lying. *Sniff* Guess what my keen nose just picked up? This smell... is the smell of...

t_ch: 是说谎的味道！
t_en: A liar!

t_ch: 哦，你很上道嘛。
t_en: Uh-huh. You know what I'm talking about, don't you?

t_ch: 是什么味道？
t_en: Of what?

t_ch: …明知故问，是说谎的味道。
t_en: ...You know what I mean. It's the smell of a liar.

t_ch: 你这人也太变态了吧！
t_en: You're a pervert!

t_ch: 呵呵，我根本不在意你的评价。
t_en: Heh, I couldn't care less about what you think of me.

t_ch: 这次就不为难你了，不过你最好小心一点，我这人脾气不好，下次就没有这么讲道理了。
t_en: I won't give you a hard time today, but be careful. I have my moods, and you might just end up on my bad side the next time I see you.

t_ch: 你也先别走，我姑且算是个劳动中介，你要是有什么本事，可以来找我试试运气。
t_en: And don't walk off. I still call myself an employment agent, so if you have any special skills, you're welcome to come and try your luck here.

t_ch: 仙舟人生活缓慢，你一个化外民也这么悠闲，有意思，想聊什么？
t_en: The Xianzhou fossils live leisurely lives, but I don't usually see Outworlders having as much free time on their hands as you. Interesting. What do you wanna talk about?

t_ch: 你具体是做什么的？
t_en: What exactly do you do?

t_ch: 劳务中介…这个词语我应该是没说错吧？就是给人介绍工作的。
t_en: I said, I'm an employment agent... I think that's the right term? I basically put people in jobs.

t_ch: 你要是缺钱想找事做，你可以来跟我混。
t_en: If you're having a case of empty pockets, then you can come and hang with me.

t_ch: 是正经的工作吗？
t_en: Are your jobs respectable?

t_ch: 正经工作多没意思呀，喂喂喂，你不会是以为我是正经人吧？
t_en: Respectable jobs? Nah, boring! Come on, don't tell me you actually think I'm a respectable agent?

t_ch: 你一个化外民在仙舟介绍工作？
t_en: You're an Outworlder putting people in Xianzhou jobs?

t_ch: 你不关心我手上有大把的工作，你干嘛关心我在哪里介绍工作呢？
t_en: Who cares about where I work my trade? Shouldn't you care more about what jobs I have available?

t_ch: 熟悉我的人都知道，真志摩介绍的工作薪水管够，关键得看有没有赚钱的本事。
t_en: Everyone knows my jobs pay plenty of dough — as long as you've got the skills to do them.

t_ch: 您既然没打算来找事做，再问下去就不礼貌了。
t_en: And if you're not looking for a job, then you'd be rude to keep asking me questions.

t_ch: 我打算要找事做。
t_en: I'm actually looking for a job.

t_ch: 那算了，我不问了。
t_en: Fine, never mind then.

t_ch: 我看人眼光还是挺准的，你很有本事，只是我现在还没有适合你的工作。
t_en: Looks like I was right about you. You've got some serious talent. Too bad I don't have any jobs for you right now.

t_ch: 你来金人巷做什么呢？
t_en: What brings you to Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 我来这里给金人巷的老街坊们找些新的活计，让他们不必再守着这些破店。
t_en: I'm here to help those good locals in Aurum Alley find better jobs, so they don't have to be stuck watching those dusty stores all day.

t_ch: 奔赴新的人生多好啊，你说是吧？
t_en: It's always great to embrace a new life, don't you think?

t_ch: 你不会是公司派来的坏家伙吧？
t_en: You're not one of those thugs working for the IPC, are you?

t_ch: 将他们赶走是有什么目的吗？
t_en: Why are you trying to get rid of them?

t_ch: 喂喂喂，你这就打探过头了，不合适吧？
t_en: Hey, hey, don't go digging where you're not supposed to. You know what I mean?

t_ch: 你和旁边这位认识吗？
t_en: Do you know the person next to you?

t_ch: 你是说雾生老～弟？哈哈哈哈，他是我最好的朋友，好到想要置之死地的程度。
t_en: You mean my man Kiryu? Hahahaha, he's my best friend! We're so tight that I want to give him a little squeeze... around the neck.

t_ch: 可惜啊，我俩是带着任务来到这里的，干掉他我也没有办法交差。
t_en: Too bad that we've got work to do. My higher-ups won't have anything nice to say if I got rid of Kiryu.

t_ch: 再说这里是仙舟罗浮，要是随随便便和他动手，只会招来地衡司或是云骑军，徒增麻烦。以后有的是机会干掉他，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Plus, I can't just go and fight him. This is the Xianzhou Luofu after all. The Realm-Keeping Commission or the Cloud Knights will be all over me. But don't worry, there'll be plenty of opportunities for me in the future. Hahahaha!

t_ch: 那雾生知道你的目的吗？
t_en: Does Kiryu know about your plans against him?

t_ch: 他当然知道，他可是我最好的朋友兼对手呀。
t_en: Of course he does! He wouldn't be my best friend and worst enemy otherwise!

t_ch: 你真的是一个怪人。
t_en: You're such a weirdo.

t_ch: 怪人？按照常人的标准来看，也许我是一个怪人。
t_en: A weirdo? If you judge me by the standards of ordinary people, then maybe.

t_ch: 但是我从不在意常人的标准。
t_en: But honestly, I've never cared about that.

t_ch: 放心吧，雾生老～弟很强的，别人早就被干掉了。
t_en: Don't worry, Kiryu is very strong. Any other person would have died ages ago.

t_ch: 以后有机会再聊吧。
t_en: I'll catch you again next time.

t_ch: 我在金人巷要待上一段时间，你要来便来，我随时都在。
t_en: I'll be staying in Aurum Alley for a while. Feel free to come back for a chat.

t_ch: 呵呵呵，你又来了。就直说吧，是什么事情要找我来着？
t_en: Hehe, here you are again. What do you need this time?

t_ch: 只是想跟你随便聊聊。
t_en: I just want to have a casual chat.

t_ch: 以后有机会再聊吧。
t_en: I'll catch you again next time.

t_ch: 商会的人要在这里谈事儿，请自觉回避。如果不够自觉的话，我可以帮你自觉。
t_en: The merchant guild will be coming here soon to discuss business. Take your leave, or I'll make you leave.

t_ch: 我又没偷听，你是自意识过剩？
t_en: I'm not eavesdropping. Aren't you just being too self-conscious?

t_ch: …自意识过剩？那不是形容小孩的词语吗？
t_en: ...Too self-conscious? Isn't that a term used to describe kids?

t_ch: 听上去口气不小，动手试试啊？
t_en: You sure talk big, buddy. You wanna fight?

t_ch: …我听说仙舟罗浮的街头治安状况良好，原来是我有些误会。
t_en: ...I've heard the Luofu streets are pretty safe, but now it seems I was mistaken.

t_ch: 商会？你也是金人巷商会的吗？
t_en: Merchant guild? Are you from the Aurum Alley Merchant Guild, too?

t_ch: 我们是…域外商会的，不是你熟悉的本地商会。
t_en: No, we are from... an Outworlder merchant guild. Not the local one you know.

t_ch: …我明白了。这不是在街道上谈论的话题，是我语气有些强硬了。
t_en: ...I see. This isn't a topic to be discussed out in the open. I was being too tough.

t_ch: 我无意与你发生口角。我叫雾生，在这人生地不熟的仙舟上做生意，难免担心「隔墙有耳」，何况这里连墙都没有，我只是比较警惕罢了。
t_en: I have no intention of arguing with you. My name is Kiryu, and I'm a foreigner doing business on the Xianzhou, where people say "the walls have ears"... not to mention there aren't even any walls here. I was just being careful.

t_ch: 你知道的，就算是无辜的陌生人，听到了不该听的内容，也会因此身陷危险…你是个有着一身本事的家伙，应该明白我是什么意思。
t_en: As you may be aware, even innocent people can get into trouble if they hear something they shouldn't... You seem to be a seasoned adventurer. I'm sure you understand what I mean.

t_ch: 话说清楚，我遇到什么危险了？
t_en: Hey, speak up. What trouble am I in?

t_ch: 这说明你没有听到什么不该听的内容。
t_en: If you're asking that question, then it means you haven't heard anything you shouldn't have.

t_ch: 这是件好事，你不必再继续打听了。
t_en: This is good news. You don't need to know anything more.

t_ch: 你还有着慧眼识人的本事啊？
t_en: You have a discerning eye, don't you?

t_ch: 毕竟我做的是劳务中介的工作，交谈两句就该把握个四五成了。
t_en: Well, I'm an employment agent, after all. I can usually get a sense of what my clients are like from just a few words.

t_ch: 我如果观察得不错，你这人足够敏锐，用不了多久，就会知道我在做什么，我有什么考虑。但你也足够聪明，我相信你知道什么事情应该插手，什么不该。
t_en: If my observation are correct, then you're sharp enough to soon figure out what I'm doing and why I do it. However, you are also sharp enough that I can trust you to know what you can get involved in, and what you shouldn't.

t_ch: …刚才就算是打完招呼了，以后有的是机会可以好好做生意。请问，还有什么要聊的吗？
t_en: ...So, we're done exchanging pleasantries. There'll be plenty of opportunities for us to do business in the future. Is there anything else you want to discuss?

t_ch: 当然，你可能听出来了，我不擅长聊天，只要你不介意的话。
t_en: You may have realized that I'm not the best at small talk, but I appreciate conversations as long as you don't mind my style of speech.

t_ch: 你具体是做什么的？
t_en: What exactly do you do?

t_ch: 劳务中介。给别人介绍工作的。
t_en: I'm an employment agent. I introduce jobs to people.

t_ch: 是正经的工作吗？
t_en: Are your jobs respectable?

t_ch: 看你对「正经工作」如何定义了。
t_en: That depends on how you define "respectable."

t_ch: 我认为我推荐的工作内容明确，薪资透明，是正经工作。
t_en: I consider the jobs I offer to be respectable because they have clear goals and fair pay.

t_ch: 劳务中介的工作有不能听的？
t_en: Are you saying an employment agent is involved in things I shouldn't know?

t_ch: 你是在小看「商业行为」。不能打听的事情太多了，不止是我安排的工作。
t_en: You underestimate "business practices." Apart from the jobs I arrange, there are many things that shouldn't be heard outside of the ears of the intended parties.

t_ch: 相信我，「劳务中介」既可以简单，也可以复杂。
t_en: Trust me, being an "employment agent" may be easy at times, but it can get complicated.

t_ch: 我只和真心找工作的人聊工作内容。你只是打听打听，聊到这种程度就够了。
t_en: I only share the details of my jobs with serious job seekers. You should already have enough to go on if you're just shopping around for opportunities.

t_ch: 你来金人巷做什么呢？
t_en: What brings you to Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 上面的工作安排而已。上面让我去金人巷，我就去金人巷。
t_en: It's just a job from the higher-ups. They told me to come, so here I am.

t_ch: 要是上面让你去反物质军团？
t_en: And what if they tell you to join the Antimatter Legion?

t_ch: 那我就去反物质军团。
t_en: Then I'll join it.

t_ch: 上面还有些什么安排呢？
t_en: What else did the high-ups tell you to do?

t_ch: 你还真是什么都问，我无可奉告。
t_en: You are too curious for your own good. I don't have anything to say to that question.

t_ch: 我说过你这人目光敏锐，要不了多久就知道我在做什么了。
t_en: As I said, I believe you're sharp enough to figure out in due time what I've been doing.

t_ch: 无关我的道德或者信仰，我只是在做上面安排的工作而已。
t_en: It has nothing to do with morals or beliefs. I'm just doing my job for the higher-ups.

t_ch: 你和旁边这位认识吗？
t_en: Do you know the person next to you?

t_ch: 你是说真志摩吗？认识。我们有着共同的恩人，恩人让我们友好相处，我尝试了。
t_en: Majima? Yes, I do. We share a common benefactor who has told us to get along well, so I gave it a try.

t_ch: 至少目前，我们两个相处得不错。她有过一段深刻的过往，克制自己、压抑自己，最后却只能等来背叛，那件事改变了她。
t_en: We're getting along pretty well for now. She has a troubled past where she had to suppress and restrain herself, only to be betrayed in the end. That experience has changed her.

t_ch: 无论她做了什么，我不会真正地怪罪于她。别看她有些疯疯癫癫的，她比大多数人都更加清醒，至少我是这样认为的。
t_en: Whatever she does, I do not blame her. She may appear to have lost her mind, but she sees the world more clearly than most people — at least, that is what I think.

t_ch: 以后有机会再聊吧。
t_en: I'll catch you again next time.

t_ch: 以后再说吧。
t_en: Catch you next time.

t_ch: 生意做久了，总能遇上些不怕死的家伙。
t_en: When you've been in the business as long as me, you'll eventually run across daredevils who do not fear death.

t_ch: 噢，我并不是在说你，请不要误会。我们之间还有什么要聊的吗？
t_en: Oh, don't get me wrong. I wasn't talking about you. Is there anything else you would like to know?

t_ch: 随便聊聊，怎样？
t_en: How about a casual chat?

t_ch: 以后有机会再聊吧。
t_en: I'll catch you again next time.

t_ch: 每天放生一本日记，只要坚持下去，我身上纠缠的业缘定会散尽，假以时日…嘿嘿……
t_en: Release a diary book every day. Keep it up, and I'll be purified of my karma. And then... Heehee...

t_ch: 光有日记可不够，还是得再买几箱「鳞渊冰泉」才好。
t_en: Just diary books won't be enough. I need to buy some crates of Scalegorge Spring Water...

t_ch: 呃，你好你好，随喜赞叹！
t_en: Um, hello hello! Praise be to happiness' dedication!

t_ch: 你在这儿嘀咕什么呢？
t_en: What were you muttering about?

t_ch: 啊？是吵到你了嘛？抱歉抱歉，我只是在念叨我的每日计划。
t_en: Ah! Was I too loud? Sorry. I was just going over my daily routine.

t_ch: 我有一个习惯，就是我每天都会放生一本日记，有时候还会额外放生些别的东西，我怕我忘了，所以就会小声提醒自己。
t_en: I have a habit of releasing a diary book every day, and sometimes I also release other things. I was just reminding myself of that in case I forget.

t_ch: 「随喜赞叹」？
t_en: "Praise be to happiness' dedication"?

t_ch: 噢，这是一句问候语，具体的含义很复杂我就不展开讲了。大概意思是「你好我好大家好，希望每个世界的大家都能过得更好」。
t_en: Oh, that's just a greeting. The actual meaning is complicated, but in simple terms, it's about wishing everyone a happy life.

t_ch: 听起来很美好对吧？这就是我所践行的放生理念。
t_en: Sounds great, right? That's why I release things.

t_ch: 放生的理念可不仅仅在仙舟上传播，还有不少化外民将它发扬光大了。只有这种超脱于物种之上的大爱，才能真正提升一个人的心境与修养。
t_en: The concept of releasing things back to nature isn't limited to the Xianzhou. It's also practiced outside of the Alliance by many Outworlders. Only a love that transcends the boundaries of species can truly elevate a person's heart and soul.

t_ch: 你好像对放生很感兴趣的，你还有什么想要了解的吗？
t_en: You seem pretty interested in releasing. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

t_ch: 你都在哪儿放生？
t_en: Where do you usually release things?

t_ch: 这个得分情况，放生讲究的是「因物制宜」，看你放生什么东西就去什么地方。
t_en: Well, it depends on the situation. Each entity has its favorite niche.

t_ch: 我个人比较喜欢去星槎海和长乐天，出入都很方便，运气好的还有机会遇到同好，说不定能学到新的放生知识。
t_en: Personally, I like Starskiff Haven and Exalting Sanctum for the convenient location. Plus, if I get lucky, I may run into fellow enthusiasts and pick up some new insights on releasing.

t_ch: 你放生过什么东西？
t_en: What have you released so far?

t_ch: 我放生过的…太多啦，那可数不清。以前刚了解放生的时候，我还放生过一些生物，好在我很快就意识到这样不好……
t_en: Well... I've released so many things already. I was releasing animals when I first started, but luckily I soon realized it was not a wise choice...

t_ch: 现在我对放生的理解深刻多了，最近的话…我在坚持放生「鳞渊冰泉」——就是矿泉水…还有日记本。
t_en: I now have a much better understanding of what it means to release things back to nature. As for now... I've stuck to only releasing Scalegorge Spring Water — you know, mineral water — and diary books.

t_ch: 这样既不会破坏当地的生态循环，又能积攒功德，于人于己都是大善事，随喜赞叹！
t_en: They won't disrupt the local ecology, and I can still accumulate virtue and remove karma! A great deed for both yourself and the environment! Praise be to happiness' dedication!

t_ch: 矿泉水也能放生？
t_en: Can mineral water be "released"?

t_ch: 当然可以啦，矿泉水本身是纯净无污染的自然物质，尽管往水里倒就行了。
t_en: Yes, of course. Mineral water is pure and natural, so you can just pour it into the water.

t_ch: 哦对了，有个细节别忘了，放生矿泉水的同时记得把瓶子捡回去，可不能乱扔垃圾。
t_en: Oh! But don't forget to bring the bottle back with you when you're done. Trash can be harmful to the environment.

t_ch: 日记本也能放生？
t_en: Can diary books be "released"?

t_ch: 听起来可能有些费解，但这是比较高深的放生理念。尽管日记本不是生命，但那上面记录了我们的生活点滴。
t_en: This may sound odd, but the action of releasing diary books sits at a much more profound level. Although a diary book is not a living creature, it is a record of our lives.

t_ch: 放生日记本，实际上就是将我们的一切烦恼与负面情绪写在日记上，借由纸页将这些情感放生出去，以此达成一种内心的圆满与平静。
t_en: By releasing it, we free ourselves from the anxiety and worries we placed onto those pages, thus achieving a sense of calmness and inner peace.

t_ch: 业缘是什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean by "karma"?

t_ch: 业缘就是我们作为人和外界一切物体之间的联系。我天天放生积攒功德，为的就是斩断业缘，保持一颗纯真的心。
t_en: Karma refers to all connections between a person and the outside world. I release things every day, so I can eventually sever these connections and purify my soul.

t_ch: 毕竟我妈从小就教育我，男孩子出门在外要多注意，要做个干净的男人！
t_en: After, my mother had taught me as a boy that males need to pay particular attention to maintaining our virtue in the adult world, and try to keep ourselves pure from the inside out!

t_ch: 放生真的有用吗？
t_en: Does "releasing" really work?

t_ch: 放生是为了消除自我的业缘，培养一种大爱，当然有用啦！
t_en: Of course it does! It's a way to purify my karma and achieve greater love!

t_ch: 我现在不就是在放生我原定的日程，在和你聊天吗？这不就是功德圆满，广结善缘嘛！
t_en: Look, I only managed to have a chat with you because of my daily release schedule. Doesn't this mean this newfound friendship is the cosmic reward for my accumulated virtue?

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好吧，其实我看你蛮有悟性的，有兴趣跟我一起放生的话可以随时来找我。
t_en: Well, you seem to have understood everything pretty quickly. If you ever want to join me in my releasing ritual, feel free to come find me.

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'd better leave you to it.

t_ch: 放生就是行最大的善，所有功德，莫过放生！
t_en: Releasing something into the wild is the greatest of good deeds! No other virtue can compare!

t_ch: 哎，你看我这跟你说话的功夫，差点忘了统计要准备的放生材料呢。好了不说了，回头再跟你聊！
t_en: Oops, I almost forgot to look over the materials for releasing. All right, maybe next time!

t_ch: 每天放生一本日记，只要坚持下去，我身上纠缠的业缘定会散尽，假以时日…嘿嘿……
t_en: Release a diary book every day. Keep it up, and I'll be purified of my karma. And then... Heehee...

t_ch: 光有日记可不够，还是得再买几箱「鳞渊冰泉」才好。
t_en: Just diary books won't be enough. I need to buy some crates of Scalegorge Spring Water...

t_ch: 咦，又是你，你是有什么事吗？
t_en: Oh, it's you again. What's up?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'd better leave you to it.

t_ch: 放生帮最近怎么样？
t_en: How is the Unshackled doing these days?

t_ch: 劳你挂念了，咱放生帮现在混得可比以前好多啦！随喜赞叹！
t_en: Thanks for your concern. We are doing so much better than before! Praise be to happiness' dedication!

t_ch: 自从我们「微观派」当了领导，能调用的资源也越来越多。以前我只能放生矿泉水，现在我直接置办了些设备，开始用生活废水搞生态养殖了。
t_en: Since we, the Microscopic Faction, took charge, we have been allocated more resources. We could only release mineral water back then. Now, we have purchased some equipment and started ecological farming with domestic wastewater.

t_ch: 这样一来，地衡司对我们也宽容了不少，甚至还邀请我参加「保护环境，节约用水」的环保活动呢！
t_en: Because of that, the Realm-Keeping Commission has become more lenient with us. They've even invited me to join the "Save Water, Save the World" event!

t_ch: 你要是有空，记得回来多参加几次放生活动啊！这可是积攒功德的大好事。哎呀，长此以往，搞不好还能洗刷掉我们这些药师信徒身上的污名。
t_en: Do come back and join our release activities if you are free! That's going to earn you lots of merits. In the long run, we might be able to clear our name as Yaoshi's followers.

t_ch: 以前仙舟人提起「药师信徒」就想起那群「药王秘传」，但以后，他们提起「药师信徒」，一定会立刻想起咱们「放生帮」！
t_en: Back then, the Xianzhou people often associated the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus with Yaoshi followers. In the future, they will think of the Unshackled at the mention of Yaoshi followers!

t_ch: 哎呀呀，随喜赞叹、随喜赞叹。
t_en: Praise be to happiness' dedication!

t_ch: 还真是和「攻略」中说的一样，不枉我费了这么多功夫……
t_en: It's just like what the guide said. It's totally worth the time...

t_ch: 这金人巷果真名不虚传，就连这空气里，满满的都是柴米油盐酱醋茶的味道。
t_en: As expected from Aurum Alley. Even its air is full of the scents of ordinary life.

t_ch: 攻略？什么攻略？
t_en: Guide? What guide?

t_ch: 就是旅游攻略啊，「三十天畅玩罗浮最全攻略」，我收藏的就是这篇。虽然热度不如别的罗浮观光攻略高，但个人认为这篇最实用。
t_en: The tourist guide, of course. "Get to Know the Real Luofu in <unbreak>30</unbreak> Days." That's the one I got. It may not be as popular as the other Luofu sightseeing guides, but I think it's the most practical one.

t_ch: 金人巷这么有名吗？
t_en: Is Aurum Alley really that famous?

t_ch: 有点名气，但不多——那种眼皮子浅只知道购物和打卡拍照的小年轻肯定不知道。但在我们这种懂行的观光客圈子里，倒算是有名。
t_en: It's relatively famous, but not by that much, and especially not for the superficial kids who only care about shopping and taking photos. However, it's a great attraction among seasoned travelers like us.

t_ch: 虽说都是吃喝玩乐，但我和某些肤浅的游客可不一样，星槎海里某些专做观光客生意的店可入不了我的眼。
t_en: Sure, I'm here to enjoy myself, but I'm different from those superficial tourists. I'm not interested in the Starskiff Haven shops that only pander to visitors.

t_ch: 说起来，我也不是第一次来仙舟了。这次来不为别的，只为感受罗浮上最地道的风土人情。
t_en: This isn't my first trip to Xianzhou. This time, I came only to experience the most authentic Luofu customs and culture.

t_ch: 你不也是游客吗？
t_en: Aren't you a tourist, too?

t_ch: 游客也分很多种，我算是经验丰富的，还写过不少攻略和点评，几年下来，在网上已经是「荣誉会员」了。这可是花钱都买不来的，懂吗？
t_en: There are all sorts of tourists, and I think of myself as a seasoned one. I've written many guides and reviews over the years, and I even got recognized as an honorary member online. You can't even buy that kind of recognition with money, you know?

t_ch: 风土人情要怎么感受？
t_en: How could one experience local customs and culture?

t_ch: 这个简单，无非是多花点时间和心思。你去哪儿玩，就和本地人多聊聊，然后去吃他们常去的本地餐厅。时间上要是赶得及，那就看看能不能赶上当地的节日和庆典活动。
t_en: It's not that hard. All you need is some time and dedication. Make sure to talk to the locals and eat at their favorite restaurants. And if you're lucky, attend local festivals and carnivals.

t_ch: 多走动、多问、多参与，千万不要窝在酒店和纪念品商店里。还有，最关键的是，尊重当地的文化习惯。出来玩，心态要放开些才是！
t_en: Walk around more, ask more questions, and join in on the fun. Don't hunker down in hotels and souvenir shops all day. And, most importantly, respect the local culture. A good traveler is always open-minded!

t_ch: 看你的打扮，应该也是外来的游客吧？你要是有观光方面的问题可以问我。
t_en: Judging by your outfit, you must be a tourist from the outside world, right? Feel free to ask me if you have any questions about sightseeing.

t_ch: 你有什么推荐的店吗？
t_en: Any recommended stores?

t_ch: 店铺啊，星槎海虽然店面挺多的，但有很多都是专做游客生意的，个人感觉不太实惠。
t_en: Stores... Well, there are lots of stores in Starskiff Haven, but many of them target tourists. I'm afraid you won't find the best deals there.

t_ch: 个人推荐金人巷里的店子，比如说那家「尚滋味」餐厅，去的好像都是罗浮本地人，好吃不贵，做法又很独特，我个人是强烈推荐你去吃吃看的。
t_en: Personally, I'd recommend the stores in Aurum Alley. For example, Spices Supreme is a restaurant only frequented by the locals. I strongly recommend you to try the unique and affordable dishes there.

t_ch: 来仙舟旅游该买什么纪念品？
t_en: What souvenirs should I buy on my Xianzhou trip?

t_ch: 纪念品…我建议你买点「苏打豆汁儿」吧，带回去给亲友派发一下，让他们尝尝，一定要让他们亲口尝尝——
t_en: Souvenirs... Well, my suggestion would be "mung bean soda." Take it back to your friends and relatives, and make sure they try it...

t_ch: 尝过以后就不会再有人来缠着你要旅游手信了，效果非常好。给自己省点事儿吧，旅行是一个人的放松，别让那些所谓的「人情世故」困住你。
t_en: Once they've tried it, they won't be pestering you for souvenirs anymore, guaranteed. I mean, come on, you're on a trip. Just relax and stop worrying about the "social obligations" back home.

t_ch: 金人巷到底有什么特别的？
t_en: What's so special about Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 怎么说呢，来这里的都是本地人为多，我看他们几家店的老板伙计好像都互相认识。除了游客没星槎海多，乍一眼看好像没什么特别的。
t_en: Well, how should I put it... Aurum Alley is frequented by locals. I swear some store owners and staff even know each other. Sure, it doesn't have as many tourists as Starskiff Haven, and looks pretty ordinary at first glance.

t_ch: 但金人巷就是有一种说不出的味道，那种…老巷子，街坊邻里的人情味，懂吧？可能这就是所谓的「烟火气」吧。
t_en: But it's got an indescribable vibe... Like the feeling of "ordinary life and everyday neighbors" in an old alley, you know?

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 嗯，你要是有什么观光心得也可以跟我交流交流。
t_en: Well, if you have any ideas to share about sightseeing, I'm all ears.

t_ch: 哎，我就喜欢这市井长巷里的烟火气，这年头可不好找，难得罗浮上还有金人巷这么有意思的地方……
t_en: I just love the atmosphere of ordinary life in this alley. It is not easy to find something as interesting as Aurum Alley these days on the Luofu...

t_ch: 还真是和「攻略」中说的一样，不枉我费了这么多功夫……
t_en: It's just like what the guide said. It's totally worth the time...

t_ch: 这金人巷果真名不虚传，就连这空气里，满满的都是柴米油盐酱醋茶的味道。
t_en: As expected from Aurum Alley. Even its air is full of the scents of ordinary life.

t_ch: 咦，又是你？你有什么事吗？你要是有什么观光方面的问题，可以问我，无任欢迎。出门在外，又都是观光客，互帮互助是应该的！
t_en: Hmm, you again? What's up? If you have any questions about sightseeing, you've come to the right person. It's only natural for us tourists to help each other out!

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 你有什么推荐的店吗？
t_en: Any recommended stores?

t_ch: 店铺啊，星槎海虽然店面挺多的，但有很多都是专做游客生意的，个人感觉不太实惠。
t_en: Stores... Well, there are lots of stores in Starskiff Haven, but many of them target tourists. I'm afraid you won't find the best deals there.

t_ch: 个人推荐金人巷里的店子，比如说那家「尚滋味」餐厅，去的好像都是罗浮本地人，好吃不贵，做法又很独特，我个人是强烈推荐你去吃吃看的。
t_en: Personally, I'd recommend the stores in Aurum Alley. For example, Spices Supreme is a restaurant only frequented by the locals. I strongly recommend you to try the unique and affordable dishes there.

t_ch: 来仙舟旅游该买什么纪念品？
t_en: What souvenirs should I buy on my Xianzhou trip?

t_ch: 纪念品…我建议你买点「苏打豆汁儿」吧，带回去给亲友派发一下，让他们尝尝，一定要让他们亲口尝尝——
t_en: Souvenirs... Well, my suggestion would be "mung bean soda." Take it back to your friends and relatives, and make sure they try it...

t_ch: 尝过以后就不会再有人来缠着你要旅游手信了，效果非常好。给自己省点事儿吧，旅行是一个人的放松，别让那些所谓的「人情世故」困住你。
t_en: Once they've tried it, they won't be pestering you for souvenirs anymore, guaranteed. I mean, come on, you're on a trip. Just relax and stop worrying about the "social obligations" back home.

t_ch: 金人巷到底有什么特别的？
t_en: What's so special about Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 怎么说呢，来这里的都是本地人为多，我看他们几家店的老板伙计好像都互相认识。除了游客没星槎海多，乍一眼看好像没什么特别的。
t_en: Well, how should I put it... Aurum Alley is frequented by locals. I swear some store owners and staff even know each other. Sure, it doesn't have as many tourists as Starskiff Haven, and looks pretty ordinary at first glance.

t_ch: 但金人巷就是有一种说不出的味道，那种…老巷子，街坊邻里的人情味，懂吧？可能这就是所谓的「烟火气」吧。
t_en: But it's got an indescribable vibe... Like the feeling of "ordinary life and everyday neighbors" in an old alley, you know?

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 嗯，你要是有什么观光心得也可以跟我交流交流。
t_en: Well, if you have any ideas to share about sightseeing, I'm all ears.

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'd better leave you to it.

t_ch: 没事，反正我是来观光的，闲着也是闲着。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm here for sightseeing anyway. I've got plenty of time on my hands.

t_ch: 哎，我就喜欢这市井长巷里的烟火气，这年头可不好找，难得罗浮上还有金人巷这么有意思的地方……
t_en: I just love the atmosphere of ordinary life in this alley. It is not easy to find something as interesting as Aurum Alley these days on the Luofu...

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'd better leave you to it.

t_ch: 这不是咱们的老主顾吗，咱们尚滋味的立命之本就是「亲民」，保准道道菜都便宜又好吃！您要是感兴趣就到店里坐坐。
t_en: Well, if it isn't my regular customer! Spices Supreme is all about making affordable and tasty dishes, so come in and take a seat!

t_ch: …或者，{F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}想和我再探讨一下美食吗？
t_en: ...Or, maybe you'd like to have another talk about food, {F#young lady}{M#young man}?

t_ch: 现在手头的事也不忙，您尽管问，我要是知道的，我尽量回答。
t_en: I'm not too busy right now, so feel free to ask me any questions. I'll do my best to satisfy your curiosity.

t_ch: 这里就是尚滋味吗？
t_en: Is this Spices Supreme?

t_ch: 没错，咱们尚滋味是咱金人巷的老字号招牌。客人您是化外民，可能听说过咱们店主打的「醒园菜」。
t_en: Right you are! You are now at Spices Supreme, one of the oldest and most famous restaurants in Aurum Alley. Even though you're an Outworlder, you might have heard of our special Xingyuan-style dishes.

t_ch: 您可以先试试脆瓜胡辣鸡丁、鲫香肉丝和陈婆豆腐这些招牌菜。这些都是历经时间考验的名菜，点这些准不会错。
t_en: You can try our trademark dishes like the crisped melon and diced chicken, pork slices in fish sauce, and Granny Chen's Tofu to start. These are all famous dishes that have withstood the test of time. You can't go wrong with them.

t_ch: 另外，每周四咱这儿会搞一个「创意星期四」，出一些特殊口味的「怪味菜」。这也是为了给仙舟的老饕们增加一些变数和乐趣。
t_en: Furthermore, we host an "Odd-taste Thursday" every Thursday, where you'll find new dishes with interesting flavors. These new dishes will bring new twists and fun to the gourmets in Luofu.

t_ch: 还是说说醒园菜吧…
t_en: Let's talk about Xingyuan-style dishes...

t_ch: 咱们这醒园菜，追求调味，不同食材定要搭配不同的调味、香料。而口味以辛辣椒麻为主、酸陈怪味皆有。
t_en: Xingyuan-style dishes are characterized by their delicate usage of seasonings, as each ingredient requires its unique blend of seasonings and spices to fully exploit its flavor. Most Xingyuan-style dishes have a hot and peppery taste, sometimes with a sour or slightly bitter touch.

t_ch: 尤其推荐这「红油乱斩牛杂」，将牛杂作为主料，进行卤制，而后切片。用辣椒油、花椒面等辅料制成红油浇在上面，可以说是质嫩味鲜、麻辣浓香！
t_en: Personally, I highly recommend this Chili Oil Beef Offal Stew. This dish is made by marinating the beef offal, slicing it, and then pouring chili oil and ground pepper over it. Its spicy and beefy aroma is simply irresistible!

t_ch: 细说一下「创意星期四」…
t_en: Please tell me more about Odd-taste Thursday...

t_ch: 这些新推出的怪味菜基本上是「试作品」，未必会好吃，但是一定可以给您带来难以忘记的全新体验。
t_en: These new weird dishes are basically "tentative works" and may not be delicious, but they will definitely give you a new experience that you won't forget.

t_ch: 这里也推荐您「油爆飞海葵」、「果木炙烤夏塔恩恐鸟翅根」、「糖球炒视肉」尝尝鲜。
t_en: If I may, I would recommend deep-fried flying sea anemone, fruitwood-grilled Shantak moa drumettes, and sugarball-fried viscorpi.

t_ch: 听说燕翠师傅身手了得？
t_en: I heard you're a skilled fighter, Master Yancui?

t_ch: 别看咱细胳膊细腿儿，咱可是「云曳旗旌流星拳」的首席大弟子。咱这路拳法可堪称仙舟外家拳之王！
t_en: Haha, I may look like a pushover, but I am the Cloudbanner Meteor Fist's chief disciple. My fists are unrivaled among the auxiliary martial art schools in Xianzhou!

t_ch: 只不过嘛，咱后来还是去做了餐饮业，没继承武馆，但终归还是留下了不少武人的傲骨呀，嘿嘿。
t_en: It's just that I went into the food business in the end, so I didn't take over the martial art school. But I've still retained the pride of a martial artist, heehee.

t_ch: 咱从不吃那些小鱼小虾，也不吃食草动物，更不吃被驯化的猛兽…咱只吃有资格与咱生死对决的动物。
t_en: I don't eat small fish and shrimp, nor herbivores, or even domesticated beasts... I only eat animals that I can fight to the death with.

t_ch: 当然啦，这只是咱个人的坚持。咱们尚滋味还是什么食材都肯提供的哈，请您不必担心。
t_en: Of course, that's just my personal mantra. The Spices Supreme serves everything. Don't worry.

t_ch: 你自己最喜欢什么菜？
t_en: What's your favorite dish?

t_ch: 有些世界栖息着无数猛兽…我印象最深的是一颗叫塔拉萨的海洋世界……
t_en: Some worlds teem with numerous ferocious beasts... My most vivid memory was of an ocean world called Thalassa...

t_ch: 那里有一种巨型的浮空兽，叫做「六眼飞鱼」。咱当时骑在它身上，足足鏖战了两三个时辰，好悬没让它给咱囫囵个儿吞下去！
t_en: It had a giant airborne beast called a six-eyed flying fish. I stood on it, and we were locked in battle for hours. It was a miracle it didn't swallow me whole!

t_ch: 「六眼飞鱼」的那个鱼翅，可别提多好吃了！那一份鱼翅煲，是咱平生吃过最好吃的……
t_en: The fins of that fish were delicious beyond compare! That fin stew was the tastiest dish I'd ever had...

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，不好意思，很少有人问我这些取材经历，不小心讲到兴头上了。
t_en: Haha, sorry, very few people ask me about my ingredient-seeking escapades, and I got carried away.

t_ch: 关于尚滋味…
t_en: About Spices Supreme...

t_ch: 您是想问咱为什么要开这么一家店吗？哎，这可就说来话长了……
t_en: You're wondering why I opened this restaurant? Well, it's a long story...

t_ch: 大概一百年前吧，星际和平公司有个卖陆地履带的子公司，搞了个什么「星际美食家协会」。他们就在银河内，给各种餐厅评定星级。
t_en: The IPC had this subsidiary company a hundred years ago which sold conveyor belts, and they had an "Interastral Gourmet Association" that went around and rated all the eateries throughout the whole cosmos.

t_ch: 一星就算是了不起的「星际严选」了，五星则会被称为「银河最佳餐厅」。整个仙舟世界唯一拿到他们五星评级的是朱明仙舟上的一家「龙宫私房菜」。
t_en: If you get one star, you're part of the "Interastral Selection." If you get five stars, then you're the "Cosmos's Finest Diner." Across all of the Xianzhou ships, the only place to ever get five stars from them is "Dragon Palace's Kitchen" on the Xianzhou Zhuming.

t_ch: 咱也是持明嘛，心想着持明同胞得到了这样的荣誉，咱高低得去捧捧场…结果去了一看，您敢信吗？喂鸟都不够吃的一点东西，他们竟敢要价几十万信用点！
t_en: Being a Vidyadhara, I thought I had to go and support the business of my kin, especially when they received such honors... But that place charged hundreds of thousands of credits for portions that wouldn't even be filling for little birds! Would you believe it?

t_ch: 那个主厨还在那里装腔作势地和来吃饭的化外民说：「我们持明生性淡泊名利，就爱吃这一口儿。」哎，真是一言难尽……
t_en: That chef even put on this pretentious air and told Outworlders, "We Vidyadhara are indifferent to the trappings of fame and fortune, which is why we eat like this." Ack, that was too much...

t_ch: 从此之后，咱就立志要开一家与他们背道而驰的饭馆：量大管饱、物美价廉！咱们这家尚滋味，就是这个梦想的结晶啦！
t_en: That's when I determined to run a restaurant that would go completely opposite to everything they stood for. I want big portions, quality food, and low prices! I'm telling ya, Spices Supreme is that dream coming true!

t_ch: 不过说实在话，您今天要是不提，咱都快忘了这些渊源了…现在咱做饭开店，就图一乐儿，别无他念。
t_en: But to be honest, if you hadn't mentioned it today, I'd have forgotten about that goal... Now I run the restaurant just to have fun. Nothing else.

t_ch: 下次再来吃吧…
t_en: I'll come back later...

t_ch: 行，都行。您啥时候来都行，咱翘首以盼！慢走啊，路上小心！
t_en: Fine. Not to worry. Any time! Can't wait! Take care! Stay safe out there!

t_ch: 我想买点吃的。
t_en: I'd like to buy some food.

t_ch: 有点儿事想问你…
t_en: There is something I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 下次再来吃吧…
t_en: I'll come back later...

t_ch: 欢迎光临，想来杯九分劲道的烈焰浓茶吗？
t_en: Welcome, customer. Would you like a mug of ninety-percent strong Flaming Potent Tea?

t_ch: 你先说清楚什么是烈焰浓茶。
t_en: You need to explain what is Flaming Potent Tea first.

t_ch: 烈焰浓茶就是可以燃烧的茶。我不是辞典，所以我想我应该不用解释什么是「燃烧」什么是「茶」了吧？
t_en: Flaming Potent Tea is a tea that is combustible. I guess I don't have to explain what "tea" and "combustible" mean, right?

t_ch: 好啊，好啊，我最喜欢品茶了。
t_en: Sure! I'm a tea-lover.

t_ch: 别怪我多嘴，咱们这里的茶和不夜侯的茶有些差别，以防万一，容我说明一下——烈焰浓茶就是可以燃烧的茶。
t_en: Please allow me to remind you that my tea is different from that made by the Sleepless Earl. In case you didn't catch it the first time, I want to emphasize this again — my Flaming Potent Tea is combustible.

t_ch: 请细说「燃烧」与「茶」。
t_en: Please elaborate what "tea" and "combustible" mean.

t_ch: 杜老板深深叹了一口气。
t_en: Boss Du lets out a deep sigh.

t_ch: …好吧，「燃烧」是指可燃物在达到燃点后与助燃物发生的化学反应。
t_en: ...All right. "Combustion" refers to the chemical reaction that occurs between a combustible material and an oxidizer when the former reaches its ignition point.

t_ch: 而「茶」…通常是指由植物制成的饮料，有不同的品种和味道。烈焰浓茶口味辛辣，制作过程中包含了发酵与蒸馏，需要注意适量饮用。
t_en: As for "tea"... it usually refers to drinks made from plants. Different teas vary in taste. Flaming Potent Tea is characterized by its pungent and burning taste, and a unique process of production that includes fermentation and distillation. Therefore, drinking too much Flaming Potent Tea may be harmful to your health.

t_ch: 别糊弄我了，你这不就是酒？
t_en: Alright. Stop muddling. This is alcohol, isn't it?

t_ch: 不，这就是茶。
t_en: No, this is tea.

t_ch: 地衡司的监管部门和税率都要求，敝庄出售的「是」且「只能是」茶。希望客人能知悉。
t_en: According to the regulations issued by the Realm-Keeping Commission, we can only sell TEA to customers. I hope you can understand, dear customer.

t_ch: 太好了，这正是我需要的。
t_en: Wonderful! This is exactly what I need.

t_ch: 你说这是茶就是茶吧。
t_en: If you say so.

t_ch: 哎，客人已经懂得烈焰浓茶的真意了——真真假假不必细究，清醒糊涂又是一天。
t_en: Good, I can tell that you've understood the essence of Flaming Potent Tea. Why bother with its name, or its true composition? Just enjoy your life and drink away all your sorrows... with tea, of course.

t_ch: 别糊弄我了，你这不就是酒？
t_en: Alright. Stop muddling. This is alcohol, isn't it?

t_ch: 不，这就是茶。
t_en: No, this is tea.

t_ch: 地衡司的监管部门和税率都要求，敝庄出售的「是」且「只能是」茶。希望客人能知悉。
t_en: According to the regulations issued by the Realm-Keeping Commission, we can only sell TEA to customers. I hope you can understand, dear customer.

t_ch: 我看客人您是第一次光临小店，我送您一杯烈焰浓茶，就当是交个朋友吧。
t_en: Since you're a new face here, please accept this cup of Flaming Potent Tea as a token of my goodwill. It's on the house.

t_ch: 别看我这小店平平无奇，整个长乐天最有魅力的顾客都会来这里喝茶。当然了，您要是感兴趣，请带着故事来小店里坐坐。
t_en: My tea house may be inconspicuous, but the most influential persons in Exalting Sanctum would come here for tea. Of course, you are free to take a seat here and share your stories.

t_ch: 不瞒您说，做我这一行，无论愿意不愿意，都得学会与人聊天，想聊点什么？
t_en: To be honest, being the owner of a tea house means you simply have to chat with your customers. So, what would you like to talk about?

t_ch: 听你这么说，你很擅长聊天？
t_en: Sounds like you're good at talking then?

t_ch: 我可没说擅长，聊天固然是有技巧的，但是这技巧并不高明，人人都能掌握。
t_en: Well, that's not what I said. Conversational skills are far from esoteric and can be easily mastered by anyone.

t_ch: 根据我的经验来看，聊天主要得看客人，几杯烈焰浓茶下肚之后，客人们总是能讲出一些真假参半的故事，而我只用稍稍回应就可以了。
t_en: But, based on my experience, the quality of the conversation is up to the customer. Once my customers had a few cups of Flaming Potent Tea, they'll start telling stories and half-truths. In that case, all I need to do is nod and play along.

t_ch: 你难道不会从旁引导话题吗？
t_en: You wouldn't lead the conversation? Even subtly?

t_ch: 茶庄里客人的闲聊大多是没有营养的话题，就像波月古海的海浪，留下的是砾石和水草，运气好的话，偶尔能捡到一枚珍珠。
t_en: No, because most gossip in a tea house doesn't deserve any serious attention. It is like stumbling across a precious pearl in the waves of the Lunarescent Depths — Nine out of ten times you would only find rubble and weeds.

t_ch: 您这说法就像是说「话题总是被人冥冥引导，有心之人得到了想要的情报」，说实话，我很少会遇到这样的情况。
t_en: You make it sound like some smart person would subtly steer conversations in their favor to get useful information out of other people. Frankly, that's hardly the case here.

t_ch: 我想，在茶庄里重要的不是聊了什么，而是聊天本身。
t_en: I think what really matters in a tea house is not the content of the conversation, but the act of holding a conversation itself.

t_ch: 然后故事全部被你记住了。
t_en: And now you've memorized all the stories.

t_ch: 你担心我会泄密，会不知分寸地把客人的故事往外说？
t_en: Are you implying that I may blabber my customers' secrets to others?

t_ch: 我在店里总是无可避免地听到客人们的故事，不过请你放心，该说的、不该说的，我心中自然有数。
t_en: It is inevitable that I hear my customers' deep and intimate secrets. But rest assured, I know well what I'm not supposed to bandy about.

t_ch: 客人要是有所担心，可以不作言语，我没有挖掘客人秘密的嗜好。
t_en: If my words don't reassure you, please feel free to stay silent. As I said, I have no interest in prying into the souls of my customers.

t_ch: 金人巷的烈焰浓茶受欢迎吗？
t_en: Is Flaming Potent Tea popular in Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 我听明白了，客人是在担心小小茶庄的生意。
t_en: Ah, dear customer. You're worried about my business.

t_ch: 金人巷里烦心事不少，有烦心事的客人都是会来杜氏茶馆的。要是有一天客人没烦恼了，那我的生意可能会有危险。
t_en: Aurum Alley is never short of troubles, and people pestered by them would naturally come to my tea house. If my customers were free of troubles one day, then my business would be busted.

t_ch: 不过那一天如果真能到来，准是好事一件，我也不会难过的。
t_en: But I wouldn't fret if that happened. Who wouldn't want a world free of troubles after all?

t_ch: 有没有听到的秘密可以分享呢？
t_en: Could you share any of your secrets with me?

t_ch: 客人知道我这人恪守秘密，还是想继续打听…那么打听也得有打听秘密的规矩，比如说秘密交换秘密？
t_en: You know that I am committed to keeping secrets, but still want to probe...? Fine, but probing has a price, too. Are you willing to share a secret of your own in exchange for one of mine?

t_ch: （随口胡说的秘密）
t_en: (Tell him a "secret" that you just made up.)

t_ch: 原来是这样，我明白了。秘密虽然只是几句话，价值却是千差万别的。
t_en: So that's how it is, I see. Secrets are nothing more than a few words, yet those words differ dramatically in value.

t_ch: 我也告诉您一个相近价值的秘密。
t_en: Then I'll tell you a secret of similar value.

t_ch: 狐人睡觉的时候，尾巴尖总是朝向一个相同的方向。
t_en: When foxians sleep, their tails always point in the same cardinal direction.

t_ch: 信不信由您了。
t_en: It's up to you to believe it or not.

t_ch: （三月七跃迁时会摔倒的故事）
t_en: (Tell him that March 7th would fall down when warp jumping.)

t_ch: 原来是这样，我明白了。看得出来，您这样浪漫的客人与容易凭空摔倒的女孩总会很熟悉。
t_en: So that's how it is. I can see a romantic customer like you getting acquainted with a girl who tends to fall down for no reason.

t_ch: 我也告诉您一个可爱的秘密。
t_en: In this case, I'll share a cute secret with you, too.

t_ch: 狐人的耳朵会反应情绪，若是有欣喜之事，就像有电流通过一样立起来。
t_en: You can tell a foxian's mood by their ears. When they feel happy, their ears will perk up as if an electric current passed through.

t_ch: 信不信由您了。
t_en: It's up to you to believe it or not.

t_ch: （一切终将消逝的宇宙真理）
t_en: (Tell him the inescapable truth that everything in this universe will eventually fade away.)

t_ch: 这么重要的秘密告诉我，真的没有关系吗？
t_en: Are you really sure you can share such a crucial secret with me?

t_ch: 我也告诉您一个沉重的秘密。
t_en: Well, if this is the case... I'll share a grave secret with you, too.

t_ch: …其实烈焰浓茶在某些世界里会被称为酒。
t_en: ...Flaming Potent Tea is called alcohol in some worlds.

t_ch: 信不信由您了。
t_en: It's up to you to believe it or not.

t_ch: 你好像喜欢做一些新的烈焰浓茶？
t_en: You're fond of inventing new flavors of Flaming Potent Tea, aren't you?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，来杜氏茶庄的客人口味不一，总有人想要品尝别处没尝过的口味。
t_en: Hahahaha, different customers have different preferences, and some of them always want to enjoy flavors they've never tried before.

t_ch: 传统的烈焰浓茶再加上一些果汁、蛋清、或者奶制品…甚至是苏打豆汁儿，就能将烈焰浓茶做成混合饮品，满足不同人的喜好了。
t_en: Add some juice, egg whites, or milk... or even mung bean soda to the traditional Flaming Potent Tea, and you'll create different mixed drinks for different preferences.

t_ch: 客人要是感兴趣，也可以来我这里试试不会售卖的新品，就当是帮我一个忙，我也会给客人相应报酬的。
t_en: If you are interested, feel free to try my latest inventions that are not yet publicly available. Do me this favor and I'll make it worth your while.

t_ch: 你不会是想害我吧？
t_en: You're not planning to hurt me, are you?

t_ch: 怎么会呢？这可是探索烈焰浓茶味觉的边界，我们是在做正确的事。
t_en: Of course not. We are doing the right thing by expanding the known sensory boundaries of Flaming Potent Tea.

t_ch: 听上去我也不亏的样子…
t_en: Well, it won't hurt, I guess...

t_ch: 当然，要是新品研发顺利，我还会感谢您呢。
t_en: Sure it won't. I would even express my gratitude if the development of new products goes well.

t_ch: 等我有了新的灵感，欢迎客人来小店试一试。
t_en: You're always welcome to try my inventions when inspiration strikes, dear customer.

t_ch: 就聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 欢迎客人常来啊。
t_en: My door is always open for you, dear customer.

t_ch: 手打烈焰浓茶，劲道十足！客人又来了，要买点茶吗，还是说想随便聊聊?
t_en: My hand-brewed Flaming Potent Tea is flavorful and strong! Thank you for your continued visits. Would you like to buy some tea or just have a chat?

t_ch: 我想买点茶尝尝…
t_en: I'd like to have some tea...

t_ch: 现在方便聊聊天吗？
t_en: Do you have time to chat now?

t_ch: 就聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
t_en: Has business been going well lately?

t_ch: 顺遂顺遂，这都是托了客人的福啊。现在金人巷里熙熙攘攘的，我这「杜氏茶庄」也越来越有名了。
t_en: Thanks to you, it's been going well. Now that the Aurum Alley is bustling with people, my teahouse is garnering fame too.

t_ch: 客人您也知道，我这小店的客人还是喜欢安静，不太希望「杜氏茶庄」太过出名…说实话，我明白客人对于私密的追求。
t_en: As you know, my customers prefer a quiet environment, so they don't want the place to get too famous... I understand their requirements for privacy.

t_ch: 接下来想充分利用店内的空间和预约限流的方式保证到店顾客的体验，我也没有本事接待太多人嘛。
t_en: Next, I'd like to make full use of the space in my store and limit the crowd through a reservation system to ensure a satisfactory experience for my customers. I'm not capable of serving too many customers at once either.

t_ch: 有什么新茶需要我测试吗？
t_en: Any new teas you want me to taste?

t_ch: 客人这么主动啊？不过很遗憾，我这儿现在没什么新品能给您测试啊…请客人放心，有了新品一定第一个通知您。
t_en: I'm surprised you're so proactive. Unfortunately, I have no new teas for you at the moment... Don't worry. I'll inform you as soon as I get new teas here.

t_ch: 最近不外出上门收旧货了，客人要是有什么旧货修理，还得到我这「陈机铺」门店上来。
t_en: I've stopped heading out to collect used goods. If you have any old gadgets that needs fixing, you'll need to bring them to my Oldies Depot shop.

t_ch: 我看客人没有带着旧货来，想必是来收收旧货，或者只是随便聊聊呢？
t_en: I don't see any old gadgets with you. You must have come here to buy some second-hand goods, or maybe you're just here to chat?

t_ch: 还真是和我聊天？你可真有意思…你想聊什么？
t_en: Really? How curious... What do you want to chat about?

t_ch: 这里就是陈机铺？
t_en: Is this Oldies Depot?

t_ch: 对，这里就是陈机铺，是我从师父那里继承来的门店。这些年他临近蜕生，便将这些差事一五一十全部交付于我，现在我是这里的店主。
t_en: Yes. This is Oldies Depot, the store I inherited from my master. He entrusted this business to me when he was about to undergo hatching rebirth a few years ago, and now I'm the owner of this store.

t_ch: 师父总是讲「天不生无用之人，地不造无用之物。」简单来说就是，没有任何一样事物与人是真正的废弃物。
t_en: He always said, "Nothing is truly worthless in the world." In other words, what one sees as junk might be treasures in the eyes of another.

t_ch: 这家小小陈机铺，就是给人心和旧物的一点自留地。总有一些难以替代的东西需要这样的旧物翻新的服务。
t_en: This little store is a place reserved for old things and weary hearts. After all, there are some things that can never be truly replaced, and they need a place to mend.

t_ch: 仙舟人为什么不喜欢修理旧物？
t_en: Why don't the Xianzhou residents repair old things?

t_ch: 仙舟上很少有传家宝…或者说，传家宝大多不是什么常用的物件。因为常用的物件寿命总是比仙舟人短很多。
t_en: There are hardly any heirlooms aboard Xianzhou... Or, to put it another way, our heirlooms aren't gonna be everyday items. Ordinary goods would never survive the long-lived Xianzhou people.

t_ch: 抛开技术日新月异的新产品不谈，即使是些锅碗瓢盆的寻常物件，它们很少有能够长存的，修修补补也都是徒劳。
t_en: Forget about all the hot new tech that comes out every week. Even mundane stuff like pots and pans rarely last forever. You can fix them, but they'll soon break again.

t_ch: 而这些「坏掉的程度」往往需要卓越的技巧以及高昂的价格…简而言之，修理并不划算，购买新的更加便宜也更加方便。
t_en: And their disrepair often requires advanced skill and a high price to get them fixed... Simply put, repairs are not cheap, and buying something new works out cheaper and more convenient.

t_ch: 于是在化外民眼中，有着几百年历史的「古董」就这样随便丢给了「收破烂」的，确实挺令人惊讶的。
t_en: That's why it raises eyebrows whenever Outworlders see an antique from centuries ago get discarded without a thought, and being taken in by us as "trash."

t_ch: 想问问你与会长的关系？
t_en: What's your relationship with the president?

t_ch: 金人巷商会会长吗…非要我说的话，也是「旧物」一件。
t_en: You mean the president of the Aurum Alley Merchant Guild, I assume? Well... he's an "old gadget" to me, if I can describe it that way.

t_ch: 会长与我的师父是旧友，得益于这两人交情，我与会长也算是熟识。
t_en: He used to be a friend of my master's, and that's how I got to know him.

t_ch: 会长在我出生之前就已经在这里了，从这个角度上来说，会长早已经是金人巷风景的一部分了。
t_en: He was already here before I was born. So, in a way, he's long been an integral part of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 没别的事了，你忙吧。
t_en: That's all. I can see you're busy.

t_ch: 好嘞，下回再见。以后要是有什么修旧物、买二手、卖废品的需求记得来找我。
t_en: Alright, catch you next time then. If you ever need any old gadgets fixed, or if you want to buy and sell second-hand stuff, come find me.

t_ch: 我要收旧物。
t_en: I want to buy some second-hand stuff.

t_ch: 只是想和你聊聊天…
t_en: That's right. I just want to chat...

t_ch: 没别的事了，你忙吧。
t_en: That's all. I can see you're busy.

t_ch: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
t_en: Has business been going well lately?

t_ch: 最近生意好得不得了啊！以前金人巷没什么游客，我做的也都是仙舟人的生意…但仙舟人嘛，你也懂，不太需要我的业务。
t_en: It's been amazing! Aurum Alley never used to have any travelers, and I only ever did business with Xianzhou locals... But, well, you know how the Xianzhou folks are like... They don't really need my services.

t_ch: 现在今非昔比啦！金人巷到处都是化外民，他们对仙舟那些成百上千年历史的「旧物」可感兴趣了。
t_en: But everything has changed, now! The Aurum Alley is full of outworlders and they love the ancient "artifacts" of Xianzhou.

t_ch: 你们化外民对旧物的价值判断和我们不同，所以周围很多朋友都暗搓搓劝我提高些价格…可旧物就是旧物嘛。
t_en: These outworlders value artifacts differently to us, so my friends keep telling me to up the prices but... well, it's just old stuff, isn't it?

t_ch: 我这辈子也没想过发大财，只有一个简单的愿望——「物尽其用」。仅此而已。
t_en: I've never dreamed of becoming rich in this life. I have just one simple wish: "to make the best use of everything." That is all.

t_ch: 哟～这不是我最喜欢的{RUBY_B#我自己}{NICKNAME}{RUBY_E#}吗？找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Why, if it isn't my favorite version of {RUBY_B#me}{NICKNAME}{RUBY_E#}. What can I do for you?

t_ch: 世间无价宝，尽在我…哟，今儿这刮的是什么风，竟把客官您给请来了？
t_en: All the most priceless treasures are in my... Oh, what a pleasant surprise! What brings you here today?

t_ch: 瞧您是个生面孔，难得来一趟金人巷吧。
t_en: You're a new face here. Never been to Aurum Alley before, I guess?

t_ch: 来来来，这边请——我这里兜售各色奇珍异宝，老板您要不要来瞧瞧？
t_en: Come in, come in! I have all sorts of wondrous treasures here! May I interest you with something?

t_ch: 奇珍异宝？好大的口气…
t_en: Wondrous treasures? You talk pretty big...

t_ch: 您有所不知，我这店是瞧着不咋地，那是因为我家不像某些星槎海的大店，净玩些虚的。
t_en: Well, well. My store may seem lackluster at first glance, but it's because I don't spend all my budget on fluffy marketing unlike those popular stores in Starskiff Haven.

t_ch: 装修成本都省下来砸在货源上，这样才能收到好东西嘛。
t_en: Cutting costs on decorations so I can buy genuine stock is the secret to getting the best stuff.

t_ch: 奇珍异宝？那岂不是很贵？
t_en: Wondrous treasures? Won't they cost a fortune?

t_ch: 哎哟，瞧您说得，没有的事。我可不是狮子大开口漫天要价的人，我们小本买卖，讲究的就是拓展客源，我要是乱开价，那还哪有回头客呀！
t_en: Ohh, that's not the case here. Everything here is sold at a fair price. We small businesses need to attract regulars, which isn't gonna happen if I set my prices sky-high!

t_ch: 再说了…您要是真觉得贵，过来瞧瞧也无妨，就当是帮我们掌掌眼嘛，无任欢迎！
t_en: Besides, if you really think my stuff is too expensive... you're always welcome to stop by, take a look, and help us set a fairer price! You're welcome either way!

t_ch: 您别拘谨，问就是了。
t_en: Don't be a stranger. Feel free to ask me anything.

t_ch: 你家店铺的特色是？
t_en: What's your store's specialty?

t_ch: 好说好说，我家卖的都是些不同寻常的珍宝，主打的就是「独一无二」，不敢说样样都是孤品，但至少在仙舟其他店里你可见不着。
t_en: Let me tell you all about it. Here you'll find nothing but the most unusual treasures, and my motto is "one-of-a-kind." I might not be able to claim that everything in the store is the only copy in the universe, but at least you won't find them anywhere else in the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 这算哪门子「奇珍异宝」？
t_en: Are these rags what you call "wondrous treasures"?

t_ch: 你别瞧着有些物什表面破旧，但来历可不简单，不是失传的手工艺品，就是得去龙潭虎穴里才能搞到的原生态药材。
t_en: These "rags" may look old and cheap, but their histories are anything but ordinary. They are either lost artifacts or precious medicines that can only be obtained from the jaws of death.

t_ch: 易得无价宝，难得有缘人呐。我这店里的东西，您要是都不满意，那说明我们缘分未到嘛。
t_en: Only their fated owner can realize their priceless value. If none of my stuff catches your eye, perhaps they are simply not destined to be yours.

t_ch: 真的有人会买吗？
t_en: Will anyone really buy your stuff?

t_ch: 嘿，瞧您这话说的，瞧不起人不是？就算散客不买，我也有地方分销啊。
t_en: Hey, what are you trying to imply? Even if my store is rarely frequented by walk-in customers, I have my channels and connections for distribution.

t_ch: 再说了，我家是出了名的专卖稀奇货，客户群稳定得很……好了好了，这些都属于商业机密，您就甭操心了。
t_en: Besides, everyone knows me as someone specializing in rare goods. I have plenty of stable customers... Never mind. Don't you worry your pretty little head about this. These are business secrets.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 行，您忙吧。
t_en: Alright. See you later.

t_ch: 行，您要是有这方面的需求再来喊我。
t_en: No problem. If you ever need anything in my line of trade, just let me know.

t_ch: 哼，我价格又没开多高…买进卖出，是生意人的本分，怎么老有人说我是「奸商」呢？我明明是无商不奸啊。
t_en: Heh, it's not like I'm squeezing my customers... Buying low and selling high is a merchant's bounden duty. They keep calling me a greedy merchant, but all merchants are greedy, aren't they?

t_ch: 哎哟，老板，又见面了。
t_en: Ah, we meet again, dear customer!

t_ch: 来来来，这边请——我这里兜售各色奇珍异宝，老板您要不要来瞧瞧？
t_en: Come in, come in! I have all sorts of wondrous treasures here! May I interest you with something?

t_ch: 我要购物！
t_en: I want to buy something!

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 没什么事了，你先忙吧。
t_en: It's nothing. I shouldn't keep you.

t_ch: 我感觉好像少了些什么？
t_en: Why does it feel like something's missing?

t_ch: 老板少了什么，我可以帮您狠狠进货。
t_en: What is it you're lacking? I can help you stock up on it big time.

t_ch: 好像没有物流规划的环节？
t_en: Is there no Logistics Planning?

t_ch: 你完全不用机巧鸟送货的吗？
t_en: Don't you use cycranes for deliveries at all?

t_ch: 啊——我明白了，老板。就是商铺建立了合作之后的那种事对吧？
t_en: Ah, I understand. It's that thing we're supposed to do after establishing a relationship with the merchants, right?

t_ch: 不过，希望老板用上我这改造的机巧鸟，和鹤运物流那些好鸟有些不一样……
t_en: I hope you can use these cycranes that I modified. They're different from the Heron Express cycranes...

t_ch: 老板用上我这机巧鸟，<color=#dbc291ff>规划的线路越长，物流评分会越高</color>，要开始吗？
t_en: With my cycranes, <color=#dbc291ff>the longer the planned route, the higher the logistic score</color>. You ready?

t_ch: 现在就开始规划吧。
t_en: Let's get the planning started.

t_ch: 让我连线，我现在就要连线。
t_en: I love planning stuff. I wanna do it now!

t_ch: 下次再说吧。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 之前说过的物流规划…
t_en: About the logistics planning we talked about...

t_ch: 老板用上我这机巧鸟，<color=#dbc291ff>规划的线路越长，物流评分会越高</color>，要开始吗？
t_en: With my cycranes, <color=#dbc291ff>the longer the planned route, the higher the logistic score</color>. You ready?

t_ch: 现在就开始规划吧。
t_en: Let's get the planning started.

t_ch: 让我连线，我现在就要连线。
t_en: I love planning stuff. I wanna do it now!

t_ch: 下次再说吧。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
t_en: Has business been going well lately?

t_ch: 托您的福，还算可以吧…嘿，这金人巷复兴以后，倒也谈不上有多么大的变化，但生意确实是越来越好做了。
t_en: Thanks to you, it's not bad... Nothing much has changed since the revival of the Aurum Alley, but my business is thriving.

t_ch: 客户群吧，还是以前那批固定班底，没怎么扩展。反倒是货源越来越丰富了。
t_en: My customer base pretty much stays the same, but the sources of my goods have become more diverse.

t_ch: 现在金人巷时不常的就有各地的域外行商到访，个个手里都捏着些不好出手的稀罕玩意儿，跟我一样藏在大衣里面呢～
t_en: Nowadays outworld traveling merchants come here from time to time, all of them with rare goods that are hard to sell tucked under their coats...

t_ch: 这句话送给您——「没有砸在手里的货，只有不会卖货的人」。像您这样有本事的人我也会向您多取取经的。
t_en: Here's a piece of wisdom for you: There are no bad goods, but only bad salespersons. As you are a capable person, I'd like to learn from you.

t_ch: 找你随便聊聊天。
t_en: I just want to chat with you.

t_ch: 好啊！我最喜欢和人聊天了～找我聊啥都行！
t_en: Okay okay~ my favorite pastime, chatting with people. You can talk with me about anything.
